zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Mary Eliza Wing於1551年出生在英國英格蘭盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆時，她的父親Thomas Henry Wing21歲，母親Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray21歲。她於1572年6月22日與Richard Browne在英國盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1614年9月12日在她的家鄉享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Mary Eliza Wing于1551年出生在英国英格兰盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆时，她的父亲Thomas Henry Wing21岁，母亲Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray21岁。她于1572年6月22日与Richard Browne在英国盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1614年9月12日在她的家乡享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wing
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Weng (Old West Scandinavian Wenge, perhaps originally a nickname from vengi ‘pillow, cushion’).

English and Scottish: habitational name from either Wing in Buckinghamshire or Wing in Rutland. The Buckinghamshire placename probably means ‘the people of Wiwa’, from an Old English personal name Wiwa + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. The Rutland placename and probably derives from Old Norse vengi ‘field’.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 榮, see   Rong   4.

variants.name: Rong
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Guang, Fang, Shuhong, Xiao, Zhao, Bai, Chen, Cheng, Ding, Guan, Guo, Han.
In some cases also Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name in Hordaland related to rong ‘stern (of a boat)’.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 容, meaning ‘tolerate’ or ‘appearance’ in Chinese: (i) from Rong (容), the name of an ancient state (location unknown). (ii) from Rong (容), the name of a post in ancient China held by officials in charge of the management of rituals, ceremonies, and music. (iii) from the personal name of Rong Cheng (容成), an official during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ), who was said to be the inventor of the first Chinese calendar. (iv) from the personal name of Zhong Rong (仲容), an official during the reign of the legendary Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ). (v) from Zi Rong (子容), style name of Nan Rong Shi (南容適), also called Nan Rong (南容), a student of Confucius (551–479 BC ) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Eliza
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Elizabeth  , first used in the 16th century, and popular in the 18th and 19th centuries as an independent given name. It was used by  George Bernard Shaw  for the main female character,  Eliza Dolittle , in his play Pygmalion (  1913 ), which was the basis for the musical and film My Fair Lady.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Mary Eliza Wing was born in 1551, in North Luffenham, Rutland, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Henry Wing, was 21 and her mother, Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray, was 21. She married Richard Browne on 22 June 1572, in North Luffenham, Rutland, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 12 September 1614, in her hometown, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Mary Eliza Wing est née en 1551, à North Luffenham, Rutland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Henry Wing, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Browne le 22 juin 1572, à North Luffenham, Rutland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 septembre 1614, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Mary Eliza Wingが1551年にイギリスイギリスルトランドノース ルフエンハムで生まれた時，父Thomas Henry Wingは21歳，母Elizabeth Cordie or Cordrayは21歳であった。1572年6月22日にイギリスルトランドノース ルフエンハムでRichard Browneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1614年9月12日に故郷で63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Mary Eliza Wing(이)가 1551년 영국 잉글랜드 루틀랜드 노르스 루펜함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Henry Wing(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1572년 6월 22일 영국 루틀랜드 노르스 루펜함에서 Richard Browne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1614년 9월 12일 그녀의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Mary Eliza Wing родилась в 1551 г. в местности норт луффенам, рутланд, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Henry Wing было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Browne 22 июня 1572 г. в местности норт луффенам, рутланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 12 сентября 1614 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 63 лет.
